commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=966b4fc39e02b4df43e30dbfe0680c7e0caae263)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a warn-only log in `handleMessageEnd` when `lastToolError` is set and the assistant's final reply is `NO_REPLY`, `REPLY_SKIP`, or `ANNOUNCE_SKIP`.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction was provided or run. Source inspection gives a clear focused reproduction path: set `lastToolError`, keep a prior `messagingToolSentTexts` value, end with raw `NO_REPLY`, and the proposed check sees the mirrored text instead of the ack.
commands.review_followup: Contributor or maintainer follow-up is needed because the patch has a concrete ordering bug and the missing real behavior proof cannot be supplied by an automated repair lane.

target.title: fix(agent): warn when silent ack follows a tool error
target.body:
## Problem

When an agent receives a `tool_result` with `is_error: true` and then responds with a silent-ack token (`NO_REPLY`, `REPLY_SKIP`, or `ANNOUNCE_SKIP`), the failure passes through the harness with no trace. The error is logged at the tool level but the subsequent silent skip is invisible — making outages look like normal silent turns.

This came up during fleet-wide debugging: agents were silently swallowing tool errors and the only signal was missing output downstream. The absence of a log entry made the pattern impossible to grep for.

## Fix

In `handleMessageEnd` (`pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.messages.ts`), after `trimmedText` is resolved, check whether:
1. `ctx.state.lastToolError` is set (a tool in the current turn errored), **and**
2. The assistant's reply is a silent-ack token (`NO_REPLY`, `REPLY_SKIP`, or `ANNOUNCE_SKIP`)

If both are true, emit a `ctx.log.warn` with the tool name and the specific ack token.

The check is warn-only (non-fatal) to preserve backward compatibility — harness behavior is unchanged, the pattern is just made visible.

## Scope

Detection-only patch. A follow-up could route the signal to a failure handler or surface it to the UI — intentionally out of scope here so this lands quickly.

## Test plan

- [ ] Trigger a tool error in a test agent turn (e.g. sandbox-denied Bash), confirm agent responds `NO_REPLY`, confirm `agent silently acked after tool error: tool=Bash ack=NO_REPLY` appears in gateway logs
- [ ] Confirm a successful tool call followed by `NO_REPLY` does **not** fire the warning (`lastToolError` is cleared on success)
- [ ] Confirm a tool error followed by a non-silent reply does **not** fire the warning

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: orihakimi
